info.title: beyond/kv
info.description: Low-latency key-value store with namespaces, TTL, revision-based conditional writes, atomic increment, and cursor-paginated key listing. All keys and values are raw bytes; values are transmitted as `application/octet-stream`. Namespaces (0–15) are independent keyspaces sharing the same physical store.

get.summary:
List keys in a namespace, optionally filtered by prefix. Results are returned in
lexicographic order. Pagination is cursor-based: when `complete` is `false`, pass the
returned `cursor` value as the `cursor` query parameter on the next request to fetch
the subsequent page. Omit `cursor` (or pass `0`) to start from the beginning.
Across multiple shards, the cursor encodes per-shard positions so fan-out is handled
transparently by the server.

parameters.name: ns
parameters.description: Namespace (0–15). Defaults to 0.

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.description: Return only keys that begin with this string. Percent-encoded. Omit to return all keys.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous `ListResponse`. Omit or pass `0` to start from the beginning.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum keys to return per page (1–1000). Defaults to 100.

200.description: Page of matching keys in lexicographic order.

get.summary:
Fetch the raw bytes stored at `key`. The value is returned as `application/octet-stream`.
Response headers carry key metadata: `X-KV-TTL` is the remaining TTL in whole seconds
(absent when the key has no expiry), `X-KV-Revision` is the current revision counter
(absent if the key was never written via `If-Match`), and `X-KV-Metadata` is the
JSON blob attached to the key (absent if none was stored).

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Key to retrieve. Percent-encoded; all bytes are valid except `\0`.

parameters.name: ns
parameters.description: Namespace (0–15). Namespaces are independent keyspaces. Defaults to 0.

200.description: Key found. Value bytes in body.

X-KV-Metadata.description: JSON metadata blob attached to the key. Absent if none was stored.

X-KV-Revision.description: Current revision counter. Absent if the key was never written via `If-Match`.

X-KV-TTL.description: Remaining TTL in whole seconds. Absent if the key has no expiry.

404.description: Key does not exist in this namespace.

put.summary: Store raw bytes at `key`. The request body is the value (`application/octet-stream`).
put.description:
**Conditional writes** — at most one condition may be active per request:
- `nx=1` — write only if the key does **not** exist; returns 409 if it does.
- `xx=1` — write only if the key **already exists**; returns 409 if it does not.
- `If-Match: <rev>` — write only if the stored revision equals `<rev>`; returns 409
  on mismatch. On success, the new revision is returned in `X-KV-Revision`.

**TTL** — set `ttl=<seconds>` (query) or `X-KV-TTL: <seconds>` (header; takes
precedence). Use `X-KV-KeepTTL: 1` to preserve an existing expiry when overwriting a
key; mutually exclusive with any TTL option.

**Atomic read-modify** — `X-KV-Return-Old: 1` returns the previous value (200) in a
single atomic swap; 204 when the key did not exist. Mutually exclusive with all
conditional-write options.

**Metadata** — `X-KV-Metadata: <json>` attaches an arbitrary JSON value to the key
(max 64 KiB serialized). Retrievable on GET via `X-KV-Metadata`.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Key to set. Percent-encoded; all bytes are valid except `\0`.

parameters.name: ns
parameters.description: Namespace (0–15). Defaults to 0.

parameters.name: nx
parameters.description: Set `nx=1` to write only if the key does **not** exist. Mutually exclusive with `xx` and `If-Match`.

parameters.name: xx
parameters.description: Set `xx=1` to write only if the key **already exists**. Mutually exclusive with `nx` and `If-Match`.

parameters.name: ttl
parameters.description: Key expiry in seconds from now. Overridden by the `X-KV-TTL` header when both are present.

parameters.name: If-Match
parameters.description: Write only if the stored revision equals this value. Returns 409 on mismatch. On success, the new revision is in `X-KV-Revision`.

parameters.name: X-KV-TTL
parameters.description: Key expiry in seconds from now. Takes precedence over the `ttl` query parameter.

parameters.name: X-KV-KeepTTL
parameters.description: Set to any value to preserve the existing TTL when overwriting a key. Mutually exclusive with `ttl` / `X-KV-TTL`.

parameters.name: X-KV-Return-Old
parameters.description: Set to any value to atomically swap and return the previous value. Returns 200 with the old bytes (or 204 if the key did not exist). Mutually exclusive with conditional writes and `X-KV-KeepTTL`.

parameters.name: X-KV-Metadata
parameters.description: Arbitrary JSON value to attach to the key (max 64 KiB serialized). Readable on GET via `X-KV-Metadata`.

requestBody.description: Raw bytes to store. Empty body is valid.

200.description: Swap succeeded. Body contains the **previous** value (`X-KV-Return-Old` path only).

204.description: Stored. Also returned by `X-KV-Return-Old` when the key did not previously exist.

X-KV-Revision.description: New revision after a successful `If-Match` write. Absent for unconditional writes.

400.description: Incompatible options (e.g. `X-KV-KeepTTL` with a TTL option, or `X-KV-Return-Old` with a conditional write).

409.description: Conditional write failed: key already exists (`nx`), does not exist (`xx`), or revision mismatch (`If-Match`).

delete.summary:
Delete `key` from the store. Idempotent — returns 204 whether or not the key existed.
Supply `If-Match: <rev>` for a conditional delete: returns 409 if the stored revision
does not match, leaving the key untouched.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Key to delete. Percent-encoded.

parameters.name: ns
parameters.description: Namespace (0–15). Defaults to 0.

parameters.name: If-Match
parameters.description: Delete only if the stored revision equals this value. Returns 409 on mismatch, leaving the key untouched.

204.description: Deleted. Also returned when the key did not exist (idempotent).

409.description: Revision mismatch — `If-Match` was supplied but the stored revision differs.

post.summary:
Atomically increment a 64-bit signed integer counter stored at `key` by `delta`
(default 1). If the key does not exist it is initialised to 0 before incrementing.
Returns the new value. Returns 400 if the stored bytes cannot be interpreted as a
decimal integer, or if the operation would overflow i64.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Counter key. Created as 0 if it does not exist.

parameters.name: ns
parameters.description: Namespace (0–15). Defaults to 0.

parameters.name: delta
parameters.description: Amount to add. May be negative for decrement. Defaults to 1.

200.description: Increment applied. Body contains the new counter value.

400.description: Stored value is not a valid decimal integer, or the result would overflow i64.

message.description: Human-readable description of what went wrong.

value.description: New counter value after applying the delta.

complete.description:
`true` when all matching keys have been returned and there are no further pages.
`false` means a `cursor` is present and more results may be fetched.

cursor.description:
Opaque pagination cursor. Pass as the `cursor` query parameter on the next request
to fetch the subsequent page. Absent when `complete` is `true`.

tags.name: kv
tags.description: Key-value operations: get, put, delete, increment, and list.
